nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (Daily 08:00)
nodes.notes: Triggers daily at 08:00 UTC. In production each supplier has its own schedule entry with sourceType, url, supplier, and pickupCountry configured. For the POC, edit node-fetch-doc to switch supplier and source type.

nodes.name: Fetch Supplier Document (PDF / Web / Excel)
nodes.notes:
Fetches the supplier T&C document. Set sourceType to 'pdf', 'web', or 'excel'.
POC default: Hertz Spain PDF. Change url + sourceType to switch supplier.
The sourceType is injected into the output JSON and drives the IF branch below.

nodes.name: Extract Text — PDF
nodes.notes: Extracts text from PDF binary using n8n built-in PDF extractor. Handles multi-page PDFs. In production: OCR applied to scanned PDFs using AWS Textract before this node.

nodes.name: Pre-process & Hash Document
nodes.notes: Works identically for all three source types — PDF, Web, and Excel extractors all produce json.text before reaching this node. Truncates to 12,000 chars, generates MD5 hash for change detection.

values.content:
You are a specialist in car rental terms and conditions extraction.
Your task is to extract exactly five specific fields from a car rental supplier T&C document.

FIELD DEFINITIONS:
1. tpl_amount — Third party liability (TPL) coverage amount.
   - If explicit amount found, extract it (e.g. "EUR 100,000,000").
   - If document says "statutory minimum" or similar, set value to "STATUTORY_MINIMUM" and confidence HIGH.
   - If not mentioned, set value to null and confidence LOW.
   - Terminology varies: "Seguro de responsabilidad civil" (ES), "Haftpflichtversicherung" (DE), "RC", "TPLI" all mean TPL.

2. grace_period_minutes — Minutes reservation is held after scheduled pickup before cancellation.
   - Extract as integer only (e.g. 60, 29). Null if absent. Never guess.
   - Terminology: "Karenzzeit" (DE), "grace period", "held for", "reserva mantenida".

3. licence_rules — What licences are accepted, rejected, or require translation/IDP.
   - Be specific: EU vs non-EU, digital, photocopies, minimum holding period.

4. payment_rules — Payment methods accepted for deposit and rental.
   - Distinguish credit vs debit. Note cards NOT accepted (Maestro, prepaid, cash).

5. cross_border_conditions — Restrictions for taking vehicle across borders.
   - Permitted zones/countries, prohibited countries, required documents, penalties.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH = explicitly stated | MEDIUM = implied | LOW = absent or unclear
Respond ONLY with valid JSON. Never fabricate. source_text = direct quote max 100 chars or null.

=== EXAMPLE 1 — Sixt / DE (explicit TPL, clear grace period) ===

Document excerpt:
"Haftpflichtversicherung mit einer maximalen Deckungssumme von EUR 100 Mio. Die maximale
Deckungssumme je geschädigter Person beläuft sich auf EUR 12 Mio.
Die 60-Minuten-Karenz: Wir halten Ihr Fahrzeug bis zu 1 Stunde bereit.
Fotokopien und digitale Dokumente werden nicht akzeptiert.
Sixt akzeptiert Kredit- und Debit-Karten von Visa, MasterCard, Amex, Diners Club, Discover, JCB.
Prepaid-Karten nicht akzeptiert. Barzahlungen nicht akzeptiert.
Zone IV verboten. Strafe: 150 EUR."

{
  "tpl_amount": {"value": "EUR 100,000,000 (max EUR 12,000,000 per person)", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "Haftpflichtversicherung mit einer maximalen Deckungssumme von EUR 100 Mio."},
  "grace_period_minutes": {"value": 60, "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "halten Ihr Fahrzeug bis zu 1 Stunde bereit"},
  "licence_rules": {"value": "Original licence only. Photocopies and digital licences not accepted. Non-German licences require certified translation.", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "Fotokopien und digitale Dokumente werden nicht akzeptiert"},
  "payment_rules": {"value": "Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Diners Club, Discover, JCB accepted. Prepaid cards not accepted. Cash not accepted.", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "Prepaid-Karten nicht akzeptiert. Barzahlungen nicht akzeptiert."},
  "cross_border_conditions": {"value": "Zone IV (outside Western/Central Europe) prohibited for all vehicles. Penalty for unauthorised travel: EUR 150.", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "Zone IV verboten. Strafe: 150 EUR."}
}

=== EXAMPLE 2 — Hertz / ES (statutory minimum TPL, absent grace period) ===

Document excerpt:
"Seguro de responsabilidad civil frente a terceros incluido automáticamente. Cumple con todos
los requisitos legales en materia de responsabilidad civil.
[No grace period information in this document]
RESERVA DE CRÉDITO/DEPÓSITO: bloquearemos una cantidad en su tarjeta de crédito o débito.
Mínimo 200 euros. MÁS 500 euros si no ha adquirido SuperCover.
Países prohibidos: todos los seguros quedarán invalidados. Indemnización repatriación: 2.317 EUR."

{
  "tpl_amount": {"value": "STATUTORY_MINIMUM", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "cumple con todos los requisitos legales en materia de responsabilidad civil"},
  "grace_period_minutes": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "licence_rules": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "payment_rules": {"value": "Credit and debit cards accepted. Minimum deposit EUR 200. Additional EUR 500 without SuperCover.", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "bloquearemos una cantidad en su tarjeta de crédito o débito. Mínimo 200 euros."},
  "cross_border_conditions": {"value": "Travel to prohibited countries invalidates all insurance. Vehicle repatriation penalty: EUR 2,317.", "confidence": "HIGH", "source_text": "todos los seguros quedarán invalidados. Indemnización repatriación: 2.317 EUR."}
}

NOTE: null + LOW is CORRECT for absent fields. Never guess or fabricate.

=== NOW EXTRACT from the following document ===

Supplier: {{ $json.supplier }} | Pickup Country: {{ $json.pickupCountry }}

Fields to extract (same rules as examples above):
1. tpl_amount  2. grace_period_minutes (integer or null)  3. licence_rules  4. payment_rules  5. cross_border_conditions

For each: value, confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), source_text (max 100 chars or null)

Document text:
{{ $json.processedText }}

Respond with ONLY this JSON:
{
  "tpl_amount": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "grace_period_minutes": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "licence_rules": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "payment_rules": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null},
  "cross_border_conditions": {"value": null, "confidence": "LOW", "source_text": null}
}

nodes.name: OpenAI GPT-4o — Extract T&C Fields
nodes.notes: Primary extraction using GPT-4o. Temperature=0 for deterministic, consistent outputs. In production: prompt caching is used for the system prompt across all extraction calls (major cost saving). Fallback to Anthropic Claude if this node fails or confidence is LOW on all fields.

nodes.name: Validate & Score Extraction
nodes.notes: Applies validation rules: grace period range check (0–480 min), counts low-confidence fields, determines if human review is required, flags if COB lookup is needed for TPL. In production: additional rules include cross-referencing known supplier policy patterns and checking for contradictions between fields.

nodes.name: TPL needs COB lookup?
nodes.notes: Routes to COB lookup if TPL was not explicitly stated in the supplier document. This is the expected case for most European suppliers — they reference statutory minimum rather than stating a number.

nodes.name: COB 2026 Statutory Minimum Lookup
nodes.notes: Looks up the statutory minimum TPL amount from the COB 2026 reference table for the pickup country. In production: this table is maintained as a database table, updated monthly by monitoring cobx.org for new editions. The COB disclaimer is included in every API response that references COB data.

nodes.name: Requires human review?
nodes.notes: Routes records with 2+ LOW confidence fields or validation flags to the human review queue. Records with HIGH or MEDIUM overall confidence proceed directly to the knowledge base.

parameters.subject: =TermsIQ: Human Review Required — {{ $json.supplier }} / {{ $json.pickupCountry }}

nodes.name: Send to Human Review Queue
nodes.notes: Sends low-confidence extractions to the content team review queue via email. In production: this routes to a dedicated review interface (web app) where the content team can see the extracted value alongside the source document, edit if needed, and approve. No extracted field goes live in the API without passing this gate.

nodes.name: Build API-ready Record
nodes.notes: Assembles the final structured record in the schema that will be written to the T&C knowledge base and served via the aggregator API. In production: this record is written to PostgreSQL keyed by supplier × pickup_country × field_type, with full version history retained.

nodes.name: Store in Knowledge Base (API)
nodes.notes: POC: writes the record to a placeholder API endpoint. In production: writes directly to the PostgreSQL knowledge base. The record is versioned — the previous value is not deleted, it is marked as superseded with an end_date timestamp.

nodes.name: Detect Source Type
nodes.notes: Detects source type from the URL or explicit override. Outputs sourceType: 'pdf' | 'web' | 'excel'. In production this is set per supplier in a config table.

nodes.name: Route by Source Type
nodes.notes: Routes to PDF, Web, or Excel extractor based on detected source type.

nodes.name: Extract Text — Web/HTML
nodes.notes: Strips HTML tags, navigation, scripts, and footers from web page content. Extracts clean readable text. In production: uses a dedicated HTML-to-text library (e.g. BeautifulSoup via a Python function node) for better fidelity.

nodes.name: Extract Text — Excel
nodes.notes: Reads all sheets from an Excel/CSV binary and converts to readable text. Sheet name is used as a section header. In production: supplier-specific column mapping handles structured Excel feeds.

nodes.name: Merge — All Source Types
nodes.notes: Merges output from PDF, Web, and Excel extractors into a single stream. All branches produce the same JSON shape: { text, sourceType, supplier, pickupCountry, url }. The downstream Pre-process node handles all three identically.
